commonProblems.h2: Common MG Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: K-Series Head Gasket Failure – Why Legacy MG Engines Overheat Prematurely
problems.rootCause: The K-Series' aluminium block and iron head expand at different rates under heat, stressing the original composite head gasket; combined with marginal coolant flow and thermostat placement, this creates hot spots that degrade the gasket over time .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Drain coolant, remove cylinder head, fit multi-layer steel (MLS) gasket kit with revised oil rail and high-tensile bolts; pressure-test cooling system | Viable only if caught early (no warping/cracking); extends life 2–4 years if cooling system maintained |

| Full head removal, machining, valve grind, new MLS gasket, oil rail, thermostat housing; includes coolant flush and system bleed | Suitable for moderate overheating with no block damage; addresses root cause but doesn't fix pre-existing block distortion |

| Supply & fit of reconditioned short block with upgraded MLS gasket, revised oil rail, balanced rotating assembly; bench-tested, 12-month warranty included | Recommended for severe overheating, spun bearings, or warped blocks; the only option when repair costs exceed 60% of replacement |

problems.recommendation: On a legacy MG worth £1,500–£3,500, a preventative MLS gasket upgrade at £400–£750 is excellent value if the engine is otherwise sound and hasn't overheated severely. However, if you've experienced coolant loss, white smoke, or oil contamination, the block may already be compromised — in which case a rebuilt engine with upgraded internals offers far better long-term reliability and resale value.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt K-Series engine prices for your MG ZR/ZS/ZT/TF

problems.group: MG ZS Timing Chain & Turbo Issues
problems.h4: MG ZS Timing Chain & Turbo Issues – Why 1.0L/1.5L Engines Lose Power
problems.rootCause: Early 15S4G/10E4E engines suffer from timing chain tensioner wear due to inadequate oil pressure during cold starts; combined with direct injection carbon buildup on intake valves, this accelerates chain stretch and can cause misfires, rattling, or catastrophic valve timing loss .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove timing cover, replace chain tensioner, guides, and sprockets; update ECU calibration per MG TSB 2022-018; includes oil change | Only viable if chain hasn't stretched beyond tolerance; requires early diagnosis before rattling becomes severe |

repairOptions.tier: | Intermediate – Full Timing Kit + Carbon Clean |
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Complete timing chain kit replacement plus walnut blasting of intake valves, PCV system update, and revised breather hose fitment | Addresses both timing and carbon issues; suitable for engines with moderate wear but no valve/piston damage |

| Supply & fit of reconditioned engine with reinforced tensioner, updated piston rings, OEM-spec injectors; 24-hour bench test, 12-month warranty | Recommended for engines with chain skip, valve contact, or high oil consumption; saves £900–£1,200 vs main dealer replacement |

problems.recommendation: If your MG ZS or MG3 is under 50,000 miles and showing early signs (cold-start rattle, slight misfire), the £450–£750 tensioner upgrade is a smart preventative investment. But once chain stretch causes timing errors or carbon buildup triggers persistent misfires, the cost of intermediate repairs approaches replacement value — making a rebuilt engine with upgraded components the more economical, reliable choice long-term.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt MG ZS 1.0/1.5 engines with upgraded timing components

problems.group: MG HS Turbo Wastegate & HPFP Failure
problems.h4: MG HS Turbo Wastegate & HPFP Failure – Forced Induction Weak Points
problems.rootCause: High-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) cam follower wear and turbo wastegate actuator sticking are common on SAIC turbo engines; sustained high boost pressure in the HS accelerates wear, while incorrect oil specification during servicing can starve the turbo of lubrication .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Remove turbo, replace wastegate actuator or HPFP cam follower; recalibrate boost pressure; use MG-approved 0W-20 oil | Viable for early-stage rattle or hesitation; requires specialist diagnostic equipment to confirm root cause |

| Fit remanufactured turbo with reinforced wastegate, upgraded HPFP (part #11110-20L4G), and revised ECU map for iSMART integration | Suitable for confirmed turbo failure or persistent HPFP codes; addresses both common failure points in one service |

| Supply & fit of reconditioned engine with upgraded turbo, reinforced HPFP, revised oil galleries; bench-tested, 12-month warranty | Recommended for catastrophic turbo failure causing oil contamination, or repeated HPFP failures; most cost-effective for high-mileage HS owners |

problems.recommendation: For MG HS owners under 60,000 miles experiencing boost loss or HPFP codes, the £400–£700 actuator/follower service is a worthwhile first step — but insist on using MG-approved oil and a specialist familiar with SAIC turbo systems. If the turbo has seized or oil has contaminated the intercooler, replacement is unavoidable; at that point, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components offers better value and warranty protection than repeated partial repairs.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt MG HS turbo engine prices with upgraded HPFP & wastegate

problems.h4: MG3 Cold-Start Stalling & Injector Coking – 1.0L/1.5L Petrol Weaknesses
problems.rootCause: Direct injection without port cleaning leads to carbon buildup on intake valves and injector tips; combined with aggressive stop-start calibration, this causes cold-start misfires, stalling, and rough idle — particularly on short-trip urban driving patterns .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Professional fuel system clean, injector flow test, ECU adaptation reset; includes updated stop-start calibration file | Effective for early-stage hesitation or rough idle; requires regular maintenance to prevent recurrence |

| Remove intake manifold, walnut-blast carbon from valves, replace coked injectors with OEM-spec units; update PCV breather system | Addresses root cause of carbon buildup; suitable for persistent misfires but no mechanical damage |

| Supply & fit of reconditioned engine with updated low-friction piston rings, revised breather system, OEM injectors; bench-tested, 12-month warranty | Recommended for engines with chronic stalling, high oil consumption, or confirmed piston ring wear; most economical for MG3s worth £2,000+ |

problems.recommendation: On a budget MG3, start with the £180–£350 injector clean and ECU reset — many stalling issues resolve with proper maintenance and updated software. But if the car has covered 50,000+ miles with frequent short trips, carbon buildup is likely advanced; at that point, investing in a rebuilt engine with revised internals prevents recurring issues and extends the car's usable life far more cost-effectively than repeated intermediate repairs.
problems.cta: → Get instant quotes for rebuilt MG3 engines with carbon-resistant upgrades

problems.h4: Oil Consumption & Piston Ring Wear – Modern MG Turbo Engine Concerns
problems.rootCause: Early-production turbo petrol engines used piston ring designs prone to coking under high heat and stop-start cycling; combined with aggressive turbo boost calibration, this accelerates oil consumption and can lead to catalytic converter damage if unaddressed .

repairOptions.tier: | Minor – PCV Update + Oil System Flush |
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Replace PCV valve and breather hoses, perform engine oil system flush, refill with MG-approved 0W-20 synthetic; update ECU oil-monitoring algorithm | May reduce consumption if caught early; requires strict adherence to 10,000-mile service intervals |

| Remove engine, replace piston rings with updated low-friction design, hone cylinders, renew gaskets; includes full reassembly and dyno test | Viable for confirmed high oil consumption (>1L/1,000 miles) with no cylinder scoring; labour-intensive but preserves original block |

repairOptions.tier: | Full Replacement – Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Rings |
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: | Supply & fit of reconditioned engine with revised piston ring set, updated oil control rails, reinforced turbo feed line; bench-tested, 12-month warranty | Recommended for engines with confirmed ring land damage, cylinder scoring, or catalytic converter contamination; most reliable long-term solution |

problems.recommendation: If your MG is consuming less than 0.5L per 1,000 miles and has under 40,000 miles, the £250–£450 PCV update and oil flush is a sensible preventative step. But once consumption exceeds 1L/1,000 miles or you see blue exhaust smoke, internal wear is advanced — and the cost of ring replacement approaches that of a rebuilt engine. For peace of mind and warranty coverage, the rebuilt option with upgraded rings delivers superior value and reliability.
problems.cta: → Compare prices for rebuilt MG turbo engines with upgraded piston rings

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your MG
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're managing a legacy K-Series head gasket concern or a modern turbo timing issue, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare transparent, specialist-backed quotes from UK MG engine experts. Every rebuilt engine in our network includes upgraded components that address the original failure point — plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Skip the main dealer premium, avoid guesswork repairs, and get back on the road with confidence.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare MG Engine Replacement Prices Now
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific MG model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: MG Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Our UK network supplies every MG replacement engine, providing indicative price ranges to help you compare specialist quotes instantly.

types.title: MG Reconditioned (Recon) Engines
types.description: A fully stripped, thoroughly cleaned and professionally inspected unit where all worn components are replaced with OEM-spec parts to restore factory reliability. Ideal for modern MG3, ZS and HS owners seeking dependable performance without main dealer markups.

types.title: MG Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A comprehensive teardown and rebuild featuring upgraded components designed to permanently resolve known weak points, such as reinforced MLS head gaskets and uprated timing chain tensioners. Delivers superior longevity and prevents repeat failures common in original factory units.

types.title: Used MG Engines
types.description: A verified, low-mileage donor unit pulled from a scrapped vehicle and compression-tested before dispatch. The most affordable route for budget-conscious owners replacing older legacy models or post-warranty vehicles where cost is the primary driver.

types.title: MG Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Built from scratch to exact factory tolerances, with every surface machined, every component replaced, and the unit rigorously bench-tested to as-new standards. The premium choice for performance variants or owners planning long-term retention.

types.title: MG Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly overhauled, cleaned and road-tested engine that addresses immediate running faults without undergoing a complete strip-down. A pragmatic, fast-turnaround solution for older MG TF, ZR or Metro projects where a full rebuild isn't economical.

types.title: MG Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: A complete replacement solution where a vetted UK specialist delivers your chosen engine, removes the failed unit, handles installation, fluids, ECU adaptation and provides a workmanship warranty.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £400 – £900 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find MG engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your MG model today.

engineSizes.h2: MG Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model

items.title: MG 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre diesel family, primarily the 19D4N code, powered MG's early diesel saloons and estates including the ZT and MG6, offering strong torque for long-distance driving but suffering from emissions-system complexity that accelerates with high-mileage urban use. These units were discontinued after 2016 as MG shifted focus to petrol and electrified powertrains
items.cta: Get quotes for MG 2.0L diesel engines
commonFailurePoints: injector wear leading to hard starts and power loss

items.title: MG 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: MG's smallest modern petrol displacement, the 1.0-litre turbo (10E4E/10X3G), powers entry-level ZS and MG3 models with lively performance but is prone to timing chain tensioner wear and turbo wastegate sticking when servicing intervals are extended or incorrect oil is used. These compact three-cylinder units are now the most common replacement request for post-2017 MGs
items.cta: Compare MG 1.0L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: oil consumption from piston ring coking

items.title: MG 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4-litre K-Series (14K4F) was MG's entry-level petrol engine for the ZR and early ZS, offering simple, lightweight performance but inheriting the K-Series' well-documented head gasket vulnerability when cooling system maintenance is neglected. These engines are now sought after by restorers and budget-conscious owners needing affordable, proven replacements
items.cta: Compare MG 1.4L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure due to differential thermal expansion

items.title: MG 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: MG's most versatile displacement, the 1.5-litre family spans naturally aspirated (15S4G/NSE) and turbocharged (15E4E/15S4U) variants across the MG3, ZS, HS and MG5, delivering balanced performance but requiring vigilant maintenance to avoid carbon buildup on direct-injection valves and timing component wear. This size accounts for the highest volume of replacement enquiries in EngineMarket's MG network
items.cta: Compare MG 1.5L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: Carbon buildup on intake valves (direct injection)

items.title: MG 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6-litre K-Series (16K4F) powered the mid-range MG TF and select ZS models, offering a sweet spot of refinement and responsiveness but sharing the legacy K-Series' susceptibility to head gasket degradation and coolant system weaknesses if overheating occurs. These engines remain popular with enthusiasts seeking original-specification replacements for classic MG roadsters
items.cta: Compare MG 1.6L petrol engine prices
commonFailurePoints: coolant loss from plastic thermostat housing

items.title: MG 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8-litre K-Series family (18K4F, 18K4K, 18N4T) is MG's most historically significant displacement, powering the ZR, ZS, ZT, TF and early MG6 with spirited performance but notorious for head gasket weakness and, in turbocharged form, oil starvation issues under hard driving. Rebuilt units with upgraded MLS gaskets and revised oil rails are the most reliable long-term solution
items.cta: Compare MG 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: MG 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre KV6 (20K4F) was a short-run V6 option for the MG ZT, delivering smooth power but suffering from complex timing chain tensioning and coolant passage design that can lead to costly failures if maintenance is deferred. These engines are now rare, with specialist rebuilds focusing on upgraded tensioners and revised coolant routing
items.cta: Compare MG 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: MG 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5-litre KV6 (25K4F/25K4N) was MG's flagship petrol engine, powering the ZT 190 and select limited editions with strong V6 character but inheriting the KV6 platform's vulnerability to timing chain stretch and coolant system corrosion if long-life coolant isn't maintained. Rebuilt units with reinforced chain components and updated coolant rails offer the most dependable path forward
items.cta: Compare MG 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: MG Electric Motors
items.description: MG's fully electric lineup (MG4, MG5 EV, ZS EV) uses permanent-magnet synchronous motors with no internal combustion displacement, offering low mechanical failure rates but requiring specialist diagnostics for inverter or bearing issues; replacement is rare and typically handled through authorised MG service centres. System / motor code(s): TZ184XS (MG4), TZ158XS (ZS EV)
items.cta: Enquire about MG electric motor repair

items.title: MG 1.5L Hybrid Engines
items.description: MG's plug-in hybrid variants (HS PHEV, ZS PHEV) pair the 1.5-litre turbo petrol engine (15E4E) with an electric motor and high-voltage battery, delivering strong combined output but requiring coordinated servicing of both combustion and electrical systems to avoid premature wear. Replacement demand is emerging as first-generation PHEVs approach warranty expiry. System / motor code(s): 15E4E + HX150 motor
items.cta: Enquire about MG 1.5L hybrid engine availability

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your MG needs? Enter your registration number and we'll match you to the exact engine code and displacement — instantly. All engines supplied with a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK MG specialists.

fuelTypes.intro: Choose your MG engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known brand‑wide issues, and UK specialist supply – from the most popular diesel workhorses to the latest electrified powertrains.
fuelTypes.closing: Not sure which fuel type your MG has? Enter your registration number above to instantly identify your model, engine code, fuel system and compatible replacement options – all backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK MG specialists.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: MG Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major MG engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK MG specialists.

entries.title: 10E4E 1.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine This three-cylinder turbo powers MG3 and ZS 1.0T variants manufactured from 2017 onward. Owners frequently report early timing chain tensioner collapse and turbo wastegate sticking due to oil dilution from short urban trips. Quality rebuilt units feature revised hydraulic tensioners, upgraded braided oil feed lines, and remapped ECU parameters for improved thermal management.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 10E4E MG engine replacement

entries.title: 15E4E 1.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Fitted across MG ZS, HS, MG5 and MG6 platforms from 2018 to present. Known for turbo actuator carbon buildup and direct injection valve clogging, often triggering limp mode and reduced fuel economy. Rebuilt replacements include walnut-blasted intake ports, upgraded electronic actuators, and reinforced piston rings to restore factory performance.

entries.title: 15S4U 1.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Found in facelift MG6 and HS Trophy trims produced between 2019 and 2024. Shares the 15E4E’s turbo reliability issues but adds higher thermal stress from increased factory boost pressures. Professionally overhauled units integrate high-flow intercoolers, revised cam phasers, and MLS head gaskets to prevent premature wear.

entries.title: 15S4G 1.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Naturally aspirated VTi-Tech unit for base MG3 and MG5 models from 2015 to present. Generally reliable but suffers from variable valve timing solenoid failure and oil pump wear in high-mileage daily drivers. Reconditioned replacements feature upgraded VVT actuators, new oil pump gears, and refreshed cylinder honing for extended lifespan.

entries.title: NSE 1.5 1.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Latest generation naturally aspirated unit for modern MG3 and MG5 facelifts launched from 2021 onward. Early reports highlight water pump bearing degradation and minor coolant seepage past the composite thermostat housing. Rebuilt units supply upgraded metal impeller water pumps, reinforced silicone hoses, and factory-spec torque-to-yield fasteners.
entries.cta: Get quotes for NSE 1.5 MG engine replacement

entries.title: 14K4F 1.4L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Core engine for MG ZR 1.4 and early TF 1.4 models manufactured between 1999 and 2005. Highly susceptible to composite head gasket blowouts and plastic thermostat housing fractures under sustained heat cycles. Rebuilt units address this with multi-layer steel (MLS) gaskets, machined alloy heads, and anodised coolant galleries.

entries.title: 16K4F 1.6L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Used in MG ZR 1.6 and ZS 1.6 variants from 1999 through 2005. Shares K-Series structural weaknesses but experiences accelerated rocker arm stud fatigue at higher RPM ranges. Quality replacements feature reinforced rocker covers, upgraded cam followers, and revised oil pump assemblies for consistent lubrication pressure.

entries.title: 18K4F 1.8L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Ubiquitous across MG F, TF, ZR, ZS and ZT platforms produced between 1995 and 2005. The most replaced MG engine due to chronic head gasket failure, oil starvation at corners, and cracked plastic throttle bodies. Professionally rebuilt versions incorporate uprated MLS gaskets, baffled sump pans, and reinforced timing chain tensioners.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 18K4F MG engine replacement

entries.title: 18K4J 1.8L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Early-spec variant fitted to MG F and Rover 200/400 platforms from 1995 to 1998. Experiences similar head gasket vulnerabilities but with older-style composite gaskets that fail prematurely under modern ethanol fuel blends. Rebuilt replacements feature updated K-Series block machining, reinforced cylinder head studs, and modern coolant flow routing.

entries.title: 18K4K 1.8L VVC Petrol
entries.description: Engine High-performance unit for MG TF 160 and ZR 160 produced from 2000 to 2005. Notorious for variable valve control (VVC) eccentric pulley wear and cam chain jump under aggressive driving conditions. Specialist rebuilds include upgraded VVC drive pulleys, reinforced timing kits, and dynamically balanced crankshafts to handle increased stress.

entries.title: 18K4M 1.8L Petrol
entries.description: Engine ZT and ZS luxury trim variant produced from 2001 to 2005 featuring revised intake manifolds and ECU tuning. Prone to inlet manifold runner motor failures and EGR valve sticking in later UK-spec compliance models. Rebuilt units supply reinforced plastic intake assemblies, upgraded EGR coolers, and fresh throttle bodies for optimal airflow.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 18K4M MG engine replacement

entries.title: 20K4F 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Entry KV6 unit for MG ZT 2.0 V6 and late Rover 75 platforms manufactured between 2001 and 2005. Suffers from oil pump drive sprocket wear and variable intake manifold flap failure, leading to sudden power loss. Quality rebuilt engines feature upgraded metal manifold flaps, high-capacity oil pumps, and reinforced main bearing caps.

entries.title: 25K4F 2.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Flagship engine for MG ZT 190 and ZS 2.5 V6 produced from 2001 to 2005. Highly prone to oil starvation at high revs causing big-end bearing spin and catastrophic crankshaft seizure with minimal warning. Specialist replacements include oversized ACL main/rod bearings, baffled oil sumps, and revised windage trays for track-ready durability.

entries.title: 25K4N 2.5L Petrol
entries.description: Engine Revised emission-spec KV6 for late ZT and MG7 models produced from 2004 to 2008. Shares the 25K4F’s bearing weaknesses but adds complex secondary air injection pump failures during cold starts. Rebuilt units delete problematic air injection components, install uprated bearing shells, and feature fully balanced rotating assemblies.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 25K4N MG engine replacement

entries.title: 19D4N 1.9L Diesel
entries.description: Engine Powers early MG6 1.9 CDTi models manufactured between 2008 and 2012. Isuzu-derived unit plagued by EGR cooler leaks, injector seat erosion, and dual-mass flywheel clutch wear. Reconditioned replacements feature reinforced injector sleeves, upgraded EGR blanking kits, and resurfaced flywheel contact surfaces for smooth operation.

entries.title: 18N4T 1.8L Turbo Petrol
entries.description: Engine Featured in MG GT and facelift MG6 models from 2012 to 2016. Experiences turbo oil feed line coking, leading to bearing failure and excessive exhaust smoke during hard acceleration. Rebuilt units include stainless steel braided turbo oil lines, revised PCV valves, and cleaned intercooler piping for sustained boost reliability.

entries.title: 15N4S 1.5L Petrol
entries.description: Hybrid Engine Plug-in hybrid drive unit for MG HS PHEV and EHS models produced from 2020 onward. Combines a standard petrol block with an integrated motor-generator; failure is typically linked to inverter cooling pump faults or hybrid battery isolator issues. Specialist replacements include upgraded high-voltage coolant pumps, reinforced flywheel couplings, and full system diagnostics.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 15N4S MG hybrid engine replacement

entries.title: 20T4F 2.0L Turbo Petrol
entries.description: Engine High-output unit for limited MG6 XPower and performance trims manufactured between 2014 and 2016. Prone to high-pressure fuel pump plunger wear and turbo boost solenoid failure under sustained track or highway use. Rebuilt replacements feature reinforced HPFP plungers, uprated blow-off valves, and strengthened connecting rods for durability. Closing line: Can’t find your engine code listed above? Use our full MG engine code directory below — every code from the last 30+ years — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.

engineCodeDirectory.closing: Can’t find your engine code listed above? Use our full MG engine code directory below — every code from the last 30+ years — or enter your registration number for an instant match to the correct replacement engine with warranty.

directory.h3: Full MG Engine Code Directory (All Years)
directory.label: “View all MG engine codes (120+ codes, 1990–2025)”
directory.intro: A comprehensive alphabetical listing of every MG engine code from the last three decades. Use the search above or enter your registration for an instant match to your specific engine. All codes link to a quote request.

engineYears.h2: MG Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When

years.preview: MGF, ZR, ZT & TF: K-Series & Rover Heritage Engines Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The mid-1990s revival of MG as a performance sub-brand of Rover placed the K-series four-cylinder and KV6 six-cylinder at the heart of the entire lineup - engines that defined the MGF, ZR, ZS and ZT but also introduced the most discussed reliability issue in modern MG history.
keyChanges: Rover K-series 1.4 and 1.8-litre four-cylinder (16v) becomes the core MG engine across MGF, ZR and ZS - a DOHC all-aluminium unit with exceptional power-to-weight ratio
VVC (Variable Valve Control) variant of the 1.8 K-series introduced in MGF VVC and ZR 160 - producing 160 bhp from 1.8 litres and making it the most performance-focused MG engine of the era
KV6 2.5-litre V6 fitted to ZT 190 and ZT-T estate - a smooth and torquey unit sharing architecture with Rover 75
Rover 4.6-litre V8 (Buick-derived) fitted to ZT 260 and ZT-T 260 - the most powerful MG road car of the modern era at the time
MG TF launched 2002 as mid-engined successor to MGF; K-series 1.6, 1.8 and 1.8 VVC carried over
Multi-layer steel (MLS) head gasket upgrade introduced for K-series from 2000 to address documented early single-layer failures
mainEngines: Rover K-series 1.4 16v - 1.4-litre DOHC four-cylinder petrol (MG ZR 105)
Rover K-series 1.8 16v - 1.8-litre DOHC four-cylinder petrol (MGF 1.8i, MG ZR 160, MG ZS 180, MG TF 1.8)
Rover K-series 1.8 VVC - 1.8-litre variable valve control petrol (MGF VVC, MG ZR 160, MG TF 160)
Rover KV6 2.5 - 2.5-litre V6 petrol (MG ZT 190, MG ZT-T 190)
Rover 4.6 V8 - 4.6-litre naturally aspirated V8 (MG ZT 260, MG ZT-T 260)
knownFor: K-series head gasket failure is the defining reliability concern of this era - caused primarily by coolant loss leading to overheating; MLS gasket and correct coolant maintenance largely mitigates the risk on well-maintained examples
VVC 1.8 is an exceptionally characterful engine for its era - free-revving and responsive, but VVC cam follower wear is documented on high-mileage examples
KV6 2.5 offers genuine refinement in the ZT saloon and estate; it is broadly robust but coolant system maintenance is as important as on the K-series four-cylinder
ZT 260 V8 is a heritage landmark - a rear-wheel-drive V8 MG saloon - but specialist support is essential given rarity and age of these units
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: MG ZR 1.8 K-series engine replacement - MGF head gasket failure engine rebuild - MG TF 1.8 engine for sale UK - MG ZT KV6 engine replacement - K-series VVC engine rebuild UK
years.cta: Search for 1995–2005 MG K-series and KV6 replacement engines - compare specialist rebuilt options from UK MG specialists

years.preview: MG ZS & XPower SV: Transition & Performance Landmark Performance Landmark
years.description: The ZS filled the mid-size gap in the MG lineup using the K-series 1.8 alongside a 2.5 KV6 option, while the short-lived XPower SV supercar represented MG's most ambitious performance engineering of the Rover era - using a Ford-sourced V8 in a De Tomaso-descended body.
keyChanges: MG ZS 1.8 and 2.5 KV6 launched 2001 - the K-series 1.8 fitted across the volume ZS lineup
XPower SV launched 2003 with Ford 4.6-litre V8 (Mustang-derived) producing 320 bhp - a hand-built supercar bearing the MG badge
XPower SV-R variant increases output to 385 bhp through revised engine mapping and induction
MG Rover collapse in April 2005 ends UK production of all K-series and KV6-engined models
mainEngines: Rover K-series 1.8 16v - 1.8-litre four-cylinder (MG ZS 120)
Rover KV6 2.5 - 2.5-litre V6 (MG ZS 180)
Ford Modular 4.6 V8 - 4.6-litre naturally aspirated V8 (MG XPower SV / SV-R)
knownFor: ZS 1.8 shares the same K-series head gasket concerns as ZR - proactive MLS gasket replacement is the standard recommendation from UK MG specialists
XPower SV is a heritage collector's item; Ford Modular V8 parts supply is good but the bespoke Italian bodywork and chassis makes structural repairs extremely specialist-intensive
KV6 2.5 in ZS 180 provides a genuinely quick and refined mid-size MG - more tolerant of neglect than the K-series four-cylinder
MG Rover collapse created an aftermarket parts void that independent specialists have steadily filled - replacement K-series supply is now well-established in the UK
years.cta: Find 2001–2005 MG ZS and XPower SV replacement engines - compare UK rebuilt K-series and V8 options from heritage MG specialists

years.preview: MG6: SAIC-Era Relaunch & Turbo Petrol Debut Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The MG6 marked the brand's formal relaunch under SAIC Motor ownership and introduced the first new MG powertrain developed under Chinese ownership - a 1.8-litre turbocharged petrol engine built at Longbridge, briefly, before full production moved to China.
keyChanges: 1.8T turbocharged petrol (10HM / 16HM) introduced as the sole MG6 powertrain - a SAIC-developed unit producing 160 bhp
MG6 GT and MG6 Magnette Saloon enter UK market from 2011 - first new MGs on sale in the UK since 2005
1.9-litre DTi diesel variant confirmed for later MG6 production but remained limited volume in the UK market
Assembly at Longbridge initially utilised; full production transferred to China from 2013
mainEngines: 10HM / 16HM 1.8T - 1.8-litre turbocharged four-cylinder petrol (MG6 GT, MG6 Saloon)
1.9 DTi - 1.9-litre turbocharged diesel (MG6 diesel, limited UK availability)
knownFor: MG6 1.8T is a capable and distinctive engine for its era but parts supply in the UK remains the primary concern - sourcing directly from SAIC/MG Motor UK or specialist importers is necessary
1.8T units are broadly reliable in regular use; turbo oil feed pipe condition is the primary inspection point on high-mileage examples
Diesel variant never reached meaningful UK volumes - replacement parts and specialist knowledge are very limited
MG6 positions MG as a genuine mid-size contender under SAIC ownership; it was the bridge product that established the brand's UK distribution network ahead of the SUV era
years.cta: Get quotes for MG6 1.8T replacement engines from UK MG specialists - compare rebuilt and remanufactured options

years.preview: MG GS & MG3: 1.0T, 1.5 NA & Volume Crossover Entry Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The MG GS crossover and the revised MG3 hatchback established the SAIC powertrain family in the UK volume market - introducing the 1.5-litre naturally aspirated four-cylinder and a 1.0-litre turbocharged three-cylinder that would become the backbone of the brand's non-EV lineup.
keyChanges: SAIC 1.5-litre naturally aspirated four-cylinder (15S4U) introduced as standard engine in MG GS and MG3 revised - the first high-volume SAIC-designed MG powertrain in UK showrooms
1.0-litre three-cylinder turbocharged petrol (10E3U) introduced in MG GS optional and MG3 from 2018 refresh - a GM-jointly developed unit
6-speed manual and CVT/DSG automatic transmission options introduced across lineup
Euro 6b emissions compliance standard across UK lineup from 2016
mainEngines: 15S4U - 1.5-litre naturally aspirated four-cylinder petrol (MG GS, MG3)
10E3U - 1.0-litre three-cylinder turbocharged petrol (MG GS, MG3)
knownFor: SAIC 1.5 naturally aspirated is a simple, dependable unit well-suited to urban and commuter use; it lacks outright performance but is praised for low running costs
1.0T three-cylinder is a jointly developed GM-SAIC unit offering a livelier driving character; three-cylinder vibration at low revs is a frequently noted owner complaint
MG3 with the 1.5 NA is widely regarded by UK owners as straightforward to maintain; routine service items are well-priced through the MG Motor UK network
Parts supply for SAIC-era MG3 and GS is now well-established through MG Motor UK dealers and growing independent specialist network
years.cta: Compare MG GS and MG3 replacement engine quotes for 2016–2020 models - MG Motor UK specialist network available

years.preview: MG ZS Gen 1 & MG HS Gen 1: 1.5T-GDI Turbocharged Era
years.description: The first-generation MG ZS SUV and MG HS launched in 2018 brought the 1.5-litre turbocharged GDI direct-injection petrol engine to the core of the UK MG lineup - a more sophisticated unit than the previous 1.5 NA and the foundation of MG's volume SUV offensive.
keyChanges: SAIC 1.5T-GDI turbocharged direct-injection four-cylinder introduced across MG ZS (2018) and MG HS (2018) - producing 106 PS in ZS and 162 PS in HS
1.0T three-cylinder continues as entry option in MG ZS SE
MG HS adds 7-speed DCT dual-clutch transmission as standard for the GDI unit
MG HS PHEV (plug-in hybrid) launched 2021 - pairing the 1.5T petrol with a 16.6 kWh battery and electric motor for 32-mile electric range
Euro 6d-temp emissions compliance across all petrol variants from 2020 MY
mainEngines: SAIC 1.5T-GDI - 1.5-litre turbocharged direct-injection four-cylinder (MG ZS, MG HS)
10E3U - 1.0-litre turbocharged three-cylinder (MG ZS SE entry)
1.5T PHEV - 1.5-litre turbo petrol + 16.6 kWh electric motor (MG HS PHEV)
knownFor: MG ZS is the UK's best-selling MG by volume - the 1.5T-GDI is broadly reliable in normal use but carbon build-up on direct-injection intake valves is a growing long-term maintenance note
HS 1.5T-GDI with 7-speed DCT has generated acceleration hesitation and DCT calibration complaints from UK owners, particularly in stop-start urban conditions

MG HS PHEV adds battery pack, electric motor and high-voltage system complexity - independent specialists are developing capability to service these units but MG dealer network remains the primary resource
Value-for-money positioning drives high UK sales volumes, meaning good parts availability and competitive independent labour rates as the fleet ages
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: MG ZS 1.5T engine replacement - MG HS 1.5T-GDI engine for sale UK - MG HS PHEV engine replacement - MG ZS engine failure UK - MG HS DCT engine problem
years.cta: Get specialist quotes for 2018–2022 MG ZS and HS 1.5T engine replacements - compare UK rebuilt options now

years.preview: MG5 EV & MG ZS EV: Electric Motor Era Begins Technology Introduction
years.description: 2020 marked MG's decisive entry into the UK electric vehicle market with the MG ZS EV and MG5 EV - the first battery electric MGs on sale in Britain, using SAIC-developed permanent magnet electric motors and a fixed-ratio single-speed drivetrain.
keyChanges: MG ZS EV launched in UK 2020 with 44.5 kWh battery and single permanent magnet synchronous motor producing 143 PS - the first electric MG on general UK sale
MG5 EV launched 2021 - the UK's first electric estate car; available in Standard Range (52.5 kWh) and Long Range (61.1 kWh) variants
Updated MG ZS EV Long Range launched 2021 with 72.6 kWh battery extending range to 273 miles WLTP
All EV models use single-speed fixed-ratio reduction gearbox - no traditional transmission oil or clutch service requirements
ICE petrol powertrain continues in parallel for MG ZS petrol - dual-powertrain model strategy established
mainEngines: SAIC Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (PMSM) 143 PS - (MG ZS EV Standard Range / Long Range)
SAIC PMSM 156 PS - (MG5 EV Standard Range)
SAIC PMSM 156 PS / 115 PS - (MG5 EV Long Range)
popularModels: MG ZS EV (Standard Range / Long Range)
knownFor: MG ZS EV and MG5 EV represent exceptional value-for-money in the early UK EV market - affordable pricing drove strong fleet and retail sales

Battery degradation over time is the primary long-term concern for early ZS EV and MG5 buyers - no widespread failure pattern has emerged but first-generation LFP and NMC cells are now reaching the 5-year mark
Electric motor and inverter units are broadly robust; UK independent EV specialists are investing in MG-compatible tooling as the fleet size grows
Software and OTA update management can require dealer visits rather than remote resolution - a noted owner frustration across the early EV lineup
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: MG ZS EV motor replacement UK - MG5 EV drivetrain replacement - MG ZS EV battery replacement - MG5 electric motor fault UK
years.cta: Get early quotes for MG ZS EV and MG5 EV electric motor and drivetrain replacements from UK EV specialists

years.preview: MG4 EV & Nebula Platform: Benchmark Electric Architecture Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: The MG4 EV launched in 2022 on SAIC's all-new Modular Scalable Platform (Nebula), introducing a purpose-built skateboard EV architecture with rear-wheel drive, 50:50 weight distribution, and multiple motor configurations - redefining the MG brand's engineering ambition globally.
keyChanges: MG4 EV debuts on Nebula MSP platform - purpose-built BEV architecture with flat One Pack battery system only 110 mm thick
Standard Range 51 kWh (125 kW / 168 PS rear motor) and Long Range 64 kWh (150 kW / 201 PS rear motor) launched simultaneously in UK
Extended Range 77 kWh (180 kW / 245 PS) introduced 2023 MY - 323-mile WLTP range, the longest of any MG to date
MG4 XPOWER dual-motor AWD launched 2023 - 150 kW front + 170 kW rear = 320 kW / 435 PS combined; 0–62 mph in 3.8 seconds
400V architecture standard; 800V capability embedded in platform for future variants
MG Cyberster roadster launched 2023 - dual-motor 544 PS variant uses evolved Nebula powertrain in a performance GT application
mainEngines: Nebula PMSM 125 kW - single rear motor (MG4 EV SE Standard Range 51 kWh)
Nebula PMSM 150 kW - single rear motor (MG4 EV SE Long Range / Trophy 64 kWh)
Nebula PMSM 180 kW - single rear motor (MG4 EV Extended Range 77 kWh)
Nebula Dual PMSM 320 kW (150 kW front + 170 kW rear) - AWD (MG4 XPOWER 64 kWh)
Nebula Dual PMSM 400 kW - AWD (MG Cyberster)
knownFor: MG4 EV is widely regarded as the benchmark value BEV in the UK market - praised by specialists and reviewers for its platform rigidity, weight distribution and ride quality
Nebula platform's modular One Pack battery design is engineered for easier future replacement compared to earlier integrated battery designs - a notable long-term ownership advantage
MG4 XPOWER torque vectoring and Dynamic Cornering Control system is a precision-engineered performance feature rarely seen at this price point
First-generation MG4 EV owners report minor software and infotainment glitches as the primary frustration - drivetrain reliability has been broadly strong across early UK fleet
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: MG4 EV motor replacement UK - MG4 XPOWER drivetrain replacement - MG4 EV battery replacement 2022 - MG4 EV extended range motor fault - MG Cyberster motor replacement
years.cta: Compare MG4 EV and MG4 XPOWER electric motor and powertrain replacement options from UK EV specialists - 12-month warranty options available

years.preview: MG ZS Hybrid+, MG3 Hybrid+, HS Gen 2 & MGS Platform: Self-Charging Hybrid Expansion Technology Introduction
years.description: From 2023, MG aggressively expanded its self-charging hybrid lineup - launching the MG3 Hybrid+, MG ZS Hybrid+ and second-generation HS - bringing 1.5-litre naturally aspirated petrol with dedicated hybrid motor architecture (DHT) to the mainstream MG range in a direct challenge to Toyota and Honda's hybrid dominance in the UK.
keyChanges: MG3 Hybrid+ launched 2024 - 1.5-litre NA petrol + DHT (Dedicated Hybrid Transmission) electric motor producing combined 194 PS; 0.9 kWh battery for EV burst mode
MG ZS Hybrid+ launched 2024 - same 1.5 NA + DHT architecture; 0.9 kWh battery, EV mode for low-speed urban driving
MG HS second generation (AS33) launched 2024 with 1.5T-GDI turbo petrol, self-charging hybrid and PHEV options
HS Gen 2 PHEV (AS33) delivers 339 PS combined from 1.5T + 40 kWh battery with circa 75-mile electric range
MG MGS platform models (MGS5, MGS6, MGS9) confirmed for UK from 2025 - a new premium MG sub-range with advanced hybrid and electric powertrains
MG IM5 and IM6 announced 2025 under the Roewe/IM sub-brand umbrella - premium all-electric models on evolved SAIC platform
mainEngines: SAIC 1.5 NA + DHT - 1.5-litre naturally aspirated hybrid (MG3 Hybrid+, MG ZS Hybrid+)
SAIC 1.5T-GDI + PHEV motor - 1.5-litre turbo petrol + 40 kWh PHEV (MG HS Gen 2 PHEV AS33)
SAIC 1.5T-GDI - 1.5-litre turbocharged petrol (MG HS Gen 2 standard)
Nebula / MSP Evolution EV motor - (MG MGS5 EV, MGS6 EV, MG4 Urban 2026)
knownFor: DHT hybrid system in MG3 Hybrid+ and ZS Hybrid+ is a precision-engineered self-charging architecture producing strong real-world fuel economy; as a new system, long-term specialist replacement data is still accumulating
MG HS Gen 2 PHEV offers the longest electric range of any MG PHEV to date - PHEV battery conditioning and high-voltage specialist tooling availability is the key variable for independent UK owners
MGS platform signals MG's move upmarket - a heritage moment for the brand as it positions above the value SUV space for the first time under SAIC ownership
Growing UK MG independent specialist network means owners outside major cities now have increasing access to non-dealer servicing at competitive rates
years.ticker: Common Replacement Enquiries: MG3 Hybrid+ engine replacement - MG ZS Hybrid+ drivetrain replacement UK - MG HS Gen 2 PHEV engine replacement - MG HS 1.5T 2024 engine for sale - MGS5 EV motor replacement
years.cta: Get ahead of replacement demand for 2023–present MG Hybrid+ and PHEV models - source specialist-backed engines and drivetrains from UK MG experts now

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your MG was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12-month warranty from UK MG specialists.

faq.h2: MG Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Quick, honest answers to the most common questions MG owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a MG engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned MG engine typically costs £1,200–£2,800 depending on displacement and condition, with K-Series units (18K4F) at the lower end and modern turbo codes (15E4E, 10E4E) commanding higher prices. Supply‑and‑fit labour adds £400–£900, while main dealers often charge 40–60% more for equivalent work. For tight budgets, used MG engines start around £400–£1,200 but carry higher risk. *Always request a breakdown showing parts, labour, VAT and delivery before committing.*
items.cta: Compare MG engine replacement prices now

items.question: What affects the cost of a MG engine replacement?
items.answer: Four factors drive pricing: engine size/code (1.0L turbo 10E4E vs 2.5L KV6 25K4F), condition (used vs reconditioned vs rebuilt with upgrades), vehicle complexity (PHEV systems require specialist isolation), and supply‑only vs supply‑and‑fit. Location matters too – London and Southeast labour rates run 20–30% higher than regional specialists. Our comparison tool surfaces transparent, like‑for‑like quotes so you see exactly what's included.
items.cta: Get quotes from UK MG engine specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my MG?
items.answer: In most cases, yes – especially if the chassis, gearbox and interior remain sound. A rebuilt MG engine with upgraded components (e.g., MLS gasket on K‑Series, revised tensioner on 10E4E) often outlasts the original unit. The alternative is purchasing a replacement vehicle, which for popular models like the MG ZS or MG3 may cost £3,000–£8,000 more than an engine swap. If your MG holds sentimental or resale value, replacement is almost always the smarter financial choice.
items.cta: Find reconditioned MG engines with comprehensive warranty cover

items.question: What's included in a MG engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: A full supply‑and‑fit package covers sourcing the correct engine code, removal of the failed unit, installation of the replacement, fresh fluids and filters, a workmanship warranty, safe disposal of the old engine, and a post‑fit test‑drive. Labour typically adds £400–£900 depending on model complexity – PHEV variants or KV6 V6 units require more time. *Always confirm whether the quote includes VAT, delivery and any ancillary parts like mounts or hoses.*
items.cta: Compare MG engine supply and fit deals

items.question: How long does a MG engine replacement take?
items.answer: For common models like the MG3 (15S4G) or MG ZS (10E4E), a like‑for‑like swap typically takes 3–5 working days once the engine is in hand. More complex units – such as the KV6 V6 (25K4F) in the ZT 190 or PHEV variants with hybrid isolation requirements – may require 5–7 days. The biggest variable is engine availability; our network stocks commonly replaced codes to minimise downtime. *Ask suppliers about current lead times before booking.*
items.cta: Use our free comparison tool to check MG engine availability

items.question: Do reconditioned MG engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes – every reconditioned MG engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty as standard. Extended 24‑month coverage is often available on premium rebuilt units. Warranty typically covers major internals: crankshaft, pistons, timing chain/belt, oil pump and cylinder head. *Always confirm exact terms, exclusions and claim process directly with your chosen supplier before purchase.*
items.cta: Find MG engines with 12‑month warranty cover

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned MG engine?
items.answer: Used MG engines (£400–£1,200) suit tight budgets, temporary fixes, or vehicles being sold soon – but carry unknown history and limited warranty. Reconditioned units (£1,200–£2,800) include new gaskets, seals, bearings and often upgraded components addressing known failures (e.g., MLS gasket on K‑Series). If your MG has a failure‑prone engine family, the reconditioned option with upgrades is strongly recommended for long‑term reliability.
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned MG engine prices

items.question: Where can I find MG engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK‑wide network of MG‑experienced specialists, from K‑Series experts in the Midlands to modern SAIC‑era technicians in the North West and Southeast. Our registration lookup instantly matches your model and engine code with nearby suppliers who stock compatible units and offer fitting. Nationwide pallet delivery is available if no local specialist holds your code.
items.cta: Get matched with MG engine specialists near you

items.question: What are the most common MG engine failures?
items.answer: Four failures dominate replacement demand: K‑Series head gasket failure (18K4F/16K4F in ZR/ZS/TF), timing chain tensioner wear on modern turbos (10E4E/15E4E in ZS/HS), turbo wastegate/actuator sticking (15E4E/18N4T), and HPFP cam follower wear on direct‑injection variants. These issues often progress to catastrophic damage – spun bearings, valve contact or oil contamination – making full replacement more cost‑effective than partial repair.
items.cta: Compare quotes for MG engines with upgraded failure‑point components

items.question: How can I get the best price on a MG engine?
items.answer: Compare at least three quotes – prices for the same engine code (e.g., 15E4E) can vary by £1,000+ across suppliers. Consider used for tight budgets or short‑term ownership; choose reconditioned with upgrades for long‑term peace of mind. If you have a trusted garage, ask about supply‑only pricing to save on fitting labour. *Always verify whether the quote includes VAT, delivery, core charge and warranty terms before committing.*
items.cta: Use our free tool to compare MG engine deals instantly
